summary: Decompiled a large portion of src/melee/mn/mnstagesw.c, the stage-switch menu code. The PR added the MnStageSwData user-data struct, extern declarations for menu animation/model resources, text/icon construction, stage selection navigation, input handling, cursor/icon animation updates, and stage-switch GObj setup. decomp.dev reported one full new function match, mnStageSw_80235DC8, plus .sdata2, and large partial improvements for mnStageSw_80236CBC, mnStageSw_80236548, fn_80236998, mnStageSw_802359C8, fn_80235F80, and mnStageSw_80235C58.

key_files.role: Only changed file; replaces placeholders with C implementations and support declarations for the stage-switch menu.

what_changed: Added #include <sysdolphin/baselib/debug.h> to support HSD_ASSERTREPORT in the new allocation path.
Introduced MnStageSwData with menu state bytes, a NUM_STAGES-sized enabled-state array, six HSD_JObj pointers at x20 through x34, padding, and HSD_Text* x40[NUM_STAGES].
Added externs for mnStageSw_803ED488, mn_804D6BB5, MenMainConSs_Top, and MenMainCursorSs_Top.
Changed mnStageSw_802359C8 from an HSD_GObj* prototype placeholder to a MnStageSwData* helper that creates two columns of stage icon text using HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC/HSD_SisLib_803A6368.
Implemented mnStageSw_80235C58 to find a valid selectable stage within the left or right column and return -1 when no enabled stage exists in that column.
Implemented mnStageSw_80235DC8, the fully matched D-pad navigation helper, including vertical wrap at 0/14 and 15/28 and left/right transfer between columns.
Implemented fn_80235F80 input handling for exiting the menu, toggling selected stages, saving stage-switch state through gm_801641E4, and navigating via D-pad buttons.
Added noinline wrappers mnStageSw_80236178_noinline and mnStageSw_802364A0_noinline around existing helpers, apparently to preserve desired codegen/call structure.

Implemented mnStageSw_80236548 to update hover and confirmed-selection visuals by hiding/showing child JOBJs, requesting animations, moving the selection marker, and applying mnStageSw_803ED488 animation-loop settings.

Implemented fn_80236998 as the per-frame process that tracks menu transitions, plays menu animations, creates/destroys text resources, hides inactive cursors, and syncs cached menu/hover/confirmed state.

Implemented mnStageSw_80236CBC to create the menu GObj, load MenMainConSs_Top, allocate/init MnStageSwData, create cursor JOBJs from MenMainCursorSs_Top for all 29 stages, attach them into two columns, and initialize the hover marker.
smart_moves: Reconstructed a concrete per-GObj user-data struct instead of continuing to treat user_data as raw bytes, making offsets like x2C/x30/x34 and the HSD_Text array reusable across multiple functions.

Kept NUM_STAGES as the central bound for the stage mapping, enabled-state cache, text array, and cursor loop, reducing duplicated magic 29 usage while preserving exact 15/14/28 column split behavior where needed.
Separated navigation lookup, input handling, visual updates, and object creation into distinct static functions matching the original address-based symbols.
Used explicit noinline wrapper functions and retained #pragma dont_inline around mnStageSw_8023593C, which is a targeted matching tactic for fragile codegen.
Mirrored the menu's two-column layout directly in code: stages 0-14 use x2C and stages 15-28 use x34, with y spacing derived from x30 - x2C.
Handled locked/unavailable stages consistently by checking gm_80164430(gm_801641CC(stage_id)) before displaying normal icons, navigating to an item, or saving state.
decomp_lessons: For menu decompilation, first recover the GObj user_data layout; once the JObj anchors, cached selection bytes, and text pointers are identified, several functions can be expressed coherently.
When a menu is visually arranged in columns, preserve the original split constants and wrap cases rather than over-generalizing; here 0/14 and 15/28 are meaningful control-flow boundaries.
SIS text creation often encodes UI layout details through JObj translations: this PR derives text x/y positions from HSD_JObjGetTranslationX/Y anchors instead of hardcoding every coordinate.
Stage availability and switch state are distinct: availability is checked through gm_80164430(gm_801641CC(stage_id)), while enabled/disabled state is read/written with gm_80164250 and gm_801641E4.

Partial matches can still be valuable when they convert many placeholders into structurally accurate C; the bot reported 75-91% matches for several newly implemented functions even though only mnStageSw_80235DC8 fully matched.
Use HSD_ASSERTREPORT plus GObj_InitUserData with HSD_Free when newly allocated menu user data belongs to a GObj lifecycle.
naming_conventions: File-local symbols retain the module prefix mnStageSw_ plus address suffix, e.g. mnStageSw_80235DC8 and mnStageSw_80236CBC.
Unknown struct fields are named by offset, e.g. x0, x1, x2, x1F, x20, x2C, x34, x40, with explicit pad_38 for layout.
Resource externs preserve asset-style names and capitalization: MenMainConSs_Top and MenMainCursorSs_Top.
Temporary noinline shims use the original function name plus _noinline, e.g. mnStageSw_80236178_noinline.
The local compile-time stage count is named NUM_STAGES and set to 29.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma dont_inline on mnStageSw_8023593C and reset afterward.
Added explicit _noinline wrapper functions around mnStageSw_80236178 and mnStageSw_802364A0 to influence call/inlining shape.
Inserted PAD_STACK(0x28) in fn_80235F80, indicating stack-shape preservation for matching.
Used u8 casts and s32 comparisons in loops and bounds checks, matching the original signed/unsigned behavior.
Preserved a goto-style check_dpad label in fn_80235F80, likely to keep branch structure close to the original.
Maintained pointer-walk patterns for stage_ids, icon, and text arrays where useful for codegen.
Split loops around 15 entries and NUM_STAGES rather than abstracting into a generic column helper, preserving original control flow and constants.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
decomp.dev reported matched code improved by +440 bytes and matched data by +48 bytes.
decomp.dev reported 2 new matches: mnStageSw_80235DC8 reached 100.00%, and .sdata2 reached 100.00%.

decomp.dev reported 7 partial improvements in main/melee/mn/mnstagesw: mnStageSw_80236CBC 81.17%, mnStageSw_80236548 91.36%, fn_80236998 82.38%, mnStageSw_802359C8 87.54%, fn_80235F80 84.29%, mnStageSw_80235C58 75.85%, and .data 72.46%.
decomp.dev also reported one unmatched-item regression: .sdata changed from 76.47% to 61.90% (-4 bytes).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching src/melee/mn/mnstagesw.c to see whether the partial matches from PR 2445 were completed.
Search for other menu files using GObj user_data structs with HSD_Text arrays and JObj anchor fields.
Search for _noinline wrapper use in melee/mn to identify recurring matching tactics.
Compare MenMainConSs_Top and MenMainCursorSs_Top usage in neighboring menu decompilation PRs.
